messages.content:
class CustomTimeDialog(simpledialog.Dialog):
    def body(self, master):
        self.timebutton_style = {"font": ("consolas", 11), 
        "fg": "white", "bg": "#3c3c3c", "relief": "flat"}
        self.title('Custom Time')
        self.time_background = "#242424"
        self.time_fg="white"

        master.configure(bg=self.time_background)
        self.configure(bg=self.time_background)

        self.ico = self.resource_path_timer("icon.ico")
        self.iconbitmap(self.ico)

        #timebox--------------------------------------------------
        ctime_frame = tk.Frame(master, bg=self.time_background)
        ctime_frame.grid(row=1, column=1)
        self.timebox = tk.Listbox(ctime_frame, height=6, width=15, font=self.timebutton_style["font"], bg=self.timebutton_style["bg"], fg="#dddddd", selectbackground="#222222")

        for time_string in ['15s', '30s', '45s', '1m', '5m', '10m']:
            self.timebox.insert(tk.END, time_string)
        self.timebox.grid(row=1, column=1)
        self.timebox.bind('<<ListboxSelect>>', self.set_spinboxes_from_selection)

        #minutebox-----------------------------------------------
        # Main frame to contain the minutes and seconds frames
        time_frame = tk.Frame(ctime_frame, bg=self.time_background)
        time_frame.grid(row=2, column=1, pady=5)# side=tk.TOP, fill=tk.X, padx=5, pady=5)

        # Create a frame for the minutes spinbox and label
        minutes_frame = tk.Frame(time_frame, bg=self.time_background)
        minutes_frame.pack(side=tk.LEFT, fill=tk.X)#, padx=5)

        tk.Label(minutes_frame, text="Minutes:", bg=self.time_background, fg=self.time_fg, font=self.timebutton_style["font"]).pack(side=tk.TOP)
        self.spin_minutes = tk.Spinbox(minutes_frame, from_=0, to=59, width=5, font=self.timebutton_style["font"])
        self.spin_minutes.pack(side=tk.TOP)

        # Create a frame for the seconds spinbox and label
        seconds_frame = tk.Frame(time_frame, bg=self.time_background)
        seconds_frame.pack(side=tk.LEFT, fill=tk.X, padx=5)

        tk.Label(seconds_frame, text="Seconds:", bg=self.time_background, fg=self.time_fg, font=self.timebutton_style["font"]).pack(side=tk.TOP)
        self.spin_seconds = tk.Spinbox(seconds_frame, from_=0, to=59, value=15, width=5, font=self.timebutton_style["font"], wrap=True)
        self.spin_seconds.pack(side=tk.TOP)

        #togsessbox-------------------------------------------------
        session = tk.LabelFrame(master, fg=self.timebutton_style["fg"], bg=self.time_background, relief=tk.FLAT)#, text="Sessions"
        session.grid(row=1, column=2)

        #togsessbox = tk.Frame(master, bg=self.time_background)
        togsess_frame = tk.Frame(session, bg=self.time_background)
        togsess_frame.grid (row=3,column=1, pady=5)
        self.togsess_mode = tk.BooleanVar(value=False)

        self.togsess_checkbox = tk.Checkbutton(togsess_frame, text="Session", 
        variable=self.togsess_mode, command=self.toggle_togsess)
        self.togsess_checkbox.configure(**self.timebutton_style, bd=0)
        self.togsess_checkbox.grid(row=1, column=1, sticky=tk.E, ipadx=12)

        self.img_togsess = tk.Spinbox(togsess_frame, width=3, from_=0, to=100, 
        font=self.timebutton_style["font"], wrap=True, x=500, value=5, state = 'disabled')
        self.img_togsess.grid(row=1, column=2)

        #sessionsbox-----------------------------------------------------------
        self.sessionlist = ['5 pics for 30s', '5 pics for 1m', '5 pics for 5m']
        self.sessionString = tk.StringVar(value=self.sessionlist)
        self.sessionbox = tk.Listbox(session, height=6, width=18, font=self.timebutton_style["font"], bg=self.timebutton_style["bg"], listvariable=self.sessionString, borderwidth=0, state = 'disabled', fg="#dddddd", selectbackground="#222222")
        self.sessionbox.grid(row=1, column=1)

        #session buttons frame------------------------------------------------------------------
        sessionbuttons = tk.Frame(session, bg=self.time_background)
        sessionbuttons.grid(row=2, column=1)
        self.add_button = tk.Button(sessionbuttons, text="+", width=3, command=self.add_session, default=tk.ACTIVE, state="disabled")
        self.add_button.config(**self.timebutton_style)
        self.add_button.grid(row=1, column=1)
        self.del_button = tk.Button(sessionbuttons, text="-", width=3, command=self.remove_session, default=tk.ACTIVE, state="disabled")
        self.del_button.config(**self.timebutton_style)
        self.del_button.grid(row=1, column=2, padx=(0,10))
        self.up_button = tk.Button(sessionbuttons, text="Ʌ", width=3, command=lambda: self.move(-1), default=tk.ACTIVE, state="disabled")
        self.up_button.config(**self.timebutton_style)
        self.up_button.grid(row=1, column=3)
        self.down_button = tk.Button(sessionbuttons, text="V", width=3, command=lambda: self.move(1), default=tk.ACTIVE, state="disabled")
        self.down_button.config(**self.timebutton_style)
        self.down_button.grid(row=1, column=4)
        return self.spin_seconds  # initial focus

    def add_session(self):
        try:
            img_togsess_value = int(self.img_togsess.get() or 0)
        except ValueError:
            img_togsess_value = 0

        try:
            minutes_input = int(self.spin_minutes.get() or 0)
        except ValueError:
            minutes_input = 0

        try:
            seconds_input = int(self.spin_seconds.get() or 0)
        except ValueError:
            seconds_input = 0

        # Convert total seconds to minutes and seconds
        minutes, seconds = divmod(minutes_input * 60 + seconds_input, 60)

        # Format the session string based on the minutes and seconds
        if minutes > 0 and seconds == 0:
            session_str = f"{img_togsess_value} pics for {minutes}m"
        elif minutes == 0 and seconds > 0:
            session_str = f"{img_togsess_value} pics for {seconds}s"
        elif minutes > 0 and seconds > 0:
            session_str = f"{img_togsess_value} pics for {minutes}m {seconds}s"
        else:
            # If both minutes and seconds are missing or 0, use 0s in the string
            session_str = f"{img_togsess_value} pics for 0s"

        # Append session string to the session list
        self.sessionlist.append(session_str)

        # Update the session string representation
        self.sessionString.set(self.sessionlist)

        # Select the newly added session in the session box
        self.sessionbox.selection_set(len(self.sessionlist) - 1)


    def remove_session(self):
        selected_index = self.sessionbox.curselection()
        if not selected_index:
            return

        selected_index = selected_index[0]
        self.sessionlist.pop(selected_index)
        self.sessionString.set(self.sessionlist)

        if selected_index < len(self.sessionlist):
            self.sessionbox.selection_set(selected_index)
        elif len(self.sessionlist) > 0:
            self.sessionbox.selection_set(len(self.sessionlist) - 1)

    def move(self, num):
        #num is -1 for up and 1 for down
        self.idxs = self.sessionbox.curselection()
        if not self.idxs:
            return
        for pos in self.idxs:
            text=self.sessionbox.get(pos) #gets text
            self.sessionbox.delete(pos) #removes text from list box
            self.sessionlist.pop(pos)  #removes text from list
            if pos==len(self.sessionlist) and num==1: pos=-1; #sets at beginning if at bottom and going down
            if pos==0 and num==-1: pos=len(self.sessionlist)+1; #sets at end if at top and going up
            self.sessionbox.insert(pos+num, text) #inserts into list box
            self.sessionlist.insert(pos+num, text) #inserts into list
            self.sessionbox.selection_set(pos+num)

    def toggle_togsess(self):
        if self.togsess_mode.get():
            self.togsess_checkbox.configure(fg="#2c2c2c", bg="#b8b8b8")
            self.img_togsess.config(state = 'normal')
            self.sessionbox.config (state = 'normal')
            self.add_button.config (state = 'normal')
            self.del_button.config (state = 'normal')
            self.up_button.config  (state = 'normal')
            self.down_button.config(state = 'normal')
        else:
            self.togsess_checkbox.configure(**self.timebutton_style)
            self.img_togsess.config(state = 'disabled')
            self.sessionbox.config (state = 'disabled')
            self.add_button.config (state = 'disabled')
            self.del_button.config (state = 'disabled')
            self.up_button.config  (state = 'disabled')
            self.down_button.config(state = 'disabled')

    def set_spinboxes_from_selection(self, event=None):
        index = self.timebox.curselection()
        if not index:
            return
        time_string = self.timebox.get(index)
        if 'm' in time_string:
            minutes = int(time_string.replace('m', ''))
            seconds = 0
        elif 's' in time_string:
            minutes = 0
            seconds = int(time_string.replace('s', ''))
        else:
            # Handle a possible unknown format
            self.bell()  # ring the bell to signal an error
            return

        self.spin_minutes.delete(0, tk.END)
        self.spin_minutes.insert(0, minutes)
        self.spin_seconds.delete(0, tk.END)
        self.spin_seconds.insert(0, seconds)
        
    def buttonbox(self):
        box = tk.Frame(self, bg=self.time_background)
        self.ok_button = tk.Button(box, text="Apply", width=16, command=self.ok, default=tk.ACTIVE)
        self.ok_button.config(**self.timebutton_style)
        self.ok_button.pack(side=tk.TOP, ipadx=5, pady=5)
        box.pack()

    def apply(self):
        if self.togsess_mode.get():
            # In session mode, store the list of session strings directly
            self.result = self.sessionlist  # Use the list directly instead of self.sessionString.get()
        else:
            # In standard mode, parse and combine minutes and seconds to a single interval in seconds
            try:
                minutes = int(self.spin_minutes.get())
                seconds = int(self.spin_seconds.get())
                self.result = minutes * 60 + seconds  # Total time in seconds
            except ValueError:
                # Fall back to a default interval as needed
                self.result = 1  # Default to 1 second

    def validate(self):
        try:
            minutes = int(self.spin_minutes.get())
            seconds = int(self.spin_seconds.get())
            return True
        except ValueError:
            self.bell()
            return False

class ImageViewer:
    def __init__(self, root):
        self.SUPPORTED_EXTENSIONS = (".png", ".jpg", ".jpeg", ".gif", ".bmp", ".JPG", ".JPEG", ".BMP", ".GIF", ".webp", ".WEBP")
        self.button_style = {"font": ("consolas", 11), "fg": "white", "bg": "#3c3c3c", "relief": "flat"}
        self.button_red = {"font": ("consolas", 11), "fg": "white", "bg": "green", "relief": "flat"}
        self.button_style_disabled = {"font": ("consolas", 11), "fg": "#505050", "bg": "#909090", "relief": "flat"}
        self.timer_style = {"font": ("consolas", 22), "fg": "white", "bg": "#3c3c3c", "relief": "flat"}
        self.tooltip_color = {"fg": "#ffffff", "bg": "#1c1c1c"}
        self.bgcolor = "#242424"
        self.button_width = 3
        #self.image_loading_queue = Queue(maxsize=5)
        self.switch_timestamps = []
        
        self.root = root
        self.root.geometry("800x600")  # Initial window size
        self.root.title("IMG Viewer")
        self.root.configure(bg=self.bgcolor)  # Set window background color
        self.root.attributes("-topmost", False)  # Make the window stay on top
        
        self.image_folder = ""
        self.selected_folder = "none"
        self.image_files = []
        self.current_image_index = 0
        self.history = []
        self.history_index = -1
        self.total_images = 0
        self.togsess_counter = 0
        self.togsess_mode = tk.BooleanVar(value=False)
        self.session_active = False
        self.session_index = 0
        self.session_image_count = 0
       
        self.main_frame = tk.Frame(self.root, bg=self.bgcolor)
        self.main_frame.pack()
        self.root.lift()
        
        self.select_folder_button = tk.Button(self.main_frame, text="Select Folder", command=self.select_folder)
        self.select_folder_button.configure(**self.button_red)
        self.select_folder_button.pack(side=tk.LEFT, padx=5, pady=10)
        ToolTip(self.select_folder_button, msg="Pick a folder", follow=True, 
        **self.tooltip_color)

        self.set_timer_button = tk.Button(self.main_frame, text="Timer", command=self.set_timer_interval)
        self.set_timer_button.configure(**self.button_style)
        self.set_timer_button.pack(side=tk.LEFT, padx=5, pady=10)
        ToolTip(self.set_timer_button, msg="Set timer interval", follow=True, 
        **self.tooltip_color)
        
        self.start_button = tk.Button(self.main_frame, text="Start", command=self.start_pause_slideshow)
        self.start_button.configure(**self.button_style_disabled)
        self.start_button.pack(side=tk.LEFT, padx=5, pady=10)
        ToolTip(self.start_button, msg="Start or pause timer", follow=True, 
        **self.tooltip_color)
        
		#image canvas
        self.canvas = tk.Canvas(self.root)
        self.is_mirrored = False
        self.is_greyscale = False
        self.is_paused = True
        self.rotated = 0
        self.vol = True
        self.beep1 = self.resource_path("beep1.wav")
        self.beep2 = self.resource_path("beep2.wav")
        self.ico = self.resource_path("icon.ico")
        self.root.iconbitmap(self.ico)

        self.grid_type="d"

        self.timer_label = tk.Label(self.root, text="15", anchor="ne") 
        self.timer_label.configure(**self.timer_style)
        self.timer_label.place(relx=1, anchor="ne", x=-0, y=0)

        self.timer_interval = 15000  # Default time interval in milliseconds (15 seconds)
        self.set_timer_interval = 15000  # Store the set timer interval
        self.timer = None  # Timer ID
		
        self.window_size = (self.root.winfo_width(), self.root.winfo_height())
        self.resize_timer = None

        self.root.bind("<Right>", self.next_image)
        self.root.bind("<Left>", self.previous_image)
        self.root.bind("<space>", self.start_pause_slideshow)
        self.root.bind("<Configure>", self.update_image_size)

    def select_folder(self):
        self.selected_folder = filedialog.askdirectory(initialdir=self.image_folder)
        if not self.selected_folder or self.selected_folder == self.image_folder:
            return
        self.image_folder = self.selected_folder
        if self.image_folder:
            image_files = os.listdir(self.image_folder) # Get all files in the selected folder
            self.image_files = [file for file in image_files if file.endswith(self.SUPPORTED_EXTENSIONS)] 
            self.history = []
            self.history_index = -1
            self.rotated = 0
            self.togsess_counter = 0
            self.total_images = len(self.image_files)
            if self.image_files:
                self.add_image_to_history()
                self.current_image_index = 0
                self.canvas.pack(fill=tk.BOTH, expand=True)
                self.select_folder_button.pack(side=tk.LEFT, padx=5, pady=5)
                self.root.title(f"IMGing in {self.image_folder} found {self.total_images} images")
                self.canvas.config(bg=self.bgcolor, highlightthickness=0)
                self.display_image()   
                self.start_button.config(**self.button_red)
                self.openimgdir.configure(**self.button_style)
                self.mirror_button.configure(**self.button_style)
                self.greyscale_button.configure(**self.button_style)
                self.rotate_button.configure(**self.button_style)
                self.grid_button.configure(**self.button_style)
                self.select_folder_button.configure(**self.button_style)
            else:
                messagebox.showinfo("No Image Files", "The selected folder does not contain any image files.")
                self.image_folder = ""
                self.root.title("IMG")

    def add_image_to_history(self):
        #code
    def set_timer_interval(self):
        
        dialog = CustomTimeDialog(self.root)
        if dialog.result is not None:
            self.session_active = dialog.togsess_mode.get()  # Ensure you're accessing the toggled state from the dialog

            if self.session_active:
                # Session mode is active, dialog.result contains the list of session strings
                self.process_session(dialog.result)
                # Set the interval to the first session's time
                self.timer_interval = self.current_session_list[0][1] * 1000
            else:
                # Session mode is inactive, dialog.result should be a numerical value 
                interval = dialog.result
                if interval < 1:  # Check if interval is less than 1 second
                    interval = 1
                self.timer_interval = interval * 1000  # Convert to milliseconds

        self.root.lift()  # Bring the main window to the top
        self.root.focus_force()  # Give focus to the main window
        
        minutes = self.timer_interval // 60000
        seconds = (self.timer_interval % 60000) // 1000
        
        if self.timer_interval <=59000:
            self.timer_label.config(text=f"{seconds}")
        else :
            self.timer_label.config(text=f"{minutes:d}:{seconds:02d}")


    def process_session(self, session_list):
        self.current_session_list = []
        for session_str in session_list:
            try:
                num_pics, time_str = session_str.split(' pics for ')
                minutes, seconds = self.parse_time(time_str)
                self.current_session_list.append((int(num_pics), minutes * 60 + seconds))
            except ValueError as e:
                # Handle the error appropriately, e.g., by logging or messaging the user
                pass  # Or whatever error handling you choose

    def parse_time(self, time_str):
        splits = time_str.split('m')
        if len(splits) > 1:
            minutes = int(splits[0])
            seconds = int(splits[1].replace('s', ''))
        else:
            minutes = 0
            seconds = int(time_str.replace('s', ''))
        return minutes, seconds

    def start_pause_slideshow(self, event=None):
        if self.image_folder == "":
            messagebox.showinfo("You haven't picked a folder", "Please select an image folder first.")
        else:
            if self.timer is None:  # Check if timer is not running
                self.display_image()
                self.start_button.config(text="Pause", **self.button_style)
                self.timer_label.configure(**self.timer_style)
                self.is_paused = False  # Set paused flag to False
                self.start_timer()
            else:
                if self.is_paused:  # Check if the slideshow is paused
                    self.start_button.config(text="Pause", **self.button_style)
                    self.timer_label.configure(**self.timer_style)
                    self.is_paused = False  # Set paused flag to False
                    self.start_timer()
                else:
                    self.pause_timer()
                    self.start_button.config(text="Start", **self.button_red)
                    self.timer_label.configure(bg="green")
                    self.is_paused = True  # Set paused flag to True
    
    def pause_timer(self):
        if self.timer is not None:
            self.root.after_cancel(self.timer)
            self.timer = None
        if self.session_active:
            self.session_index = 0
            self.session_image_count = self.current_session_list[0][0]
            self.timer_interval = self.current_session_list[0][1] * 1000
    def start_timer(self):
        if self.image_folder != "" and not self.is_paused:
            self.update_timer()
            self.timer = self.root.after(1000, self.start_timer)

    def update_timer(self):
        # Calculate minutes and seconds left
        minutes = self.timer_interval // 60000
        seconds = (self.timer_interval % 60000) // 1000

        if self.timer_interval <=59000:
            self.timer_label.config(text=f"{seconds}")
        else :
            self.timer_label.config(text=f"{minutes:d}:{seconds:02d}")

        if self.vol:
            if self.timer_interval == 3000:
                wave_obj = sa.WaveObject.from_wave_file(self.beep1)
                play_obj = wave_obj.play()
                play_obj.wait_done()
            if self.timer_interval == 2000:
                wave_obj = sa.WaveObject.from_wave_file(self.beep1)
                play_obj = wave_obj.play()
                play_obj.wait_done()
            if self.timer_interval == 1000:
                wave_obj = sa.WaveObject.from_wave_file(self.beep1)
                play_obj = wave_obj.play()
                play_obj.wait_done()
            if self.timer_interval == 0:
                wave_obj = sa.WaveObject.from_wave_file(self.beep2)
                play_obj = wave_obj.play()
                play_obj.wait_done()

        self.timer_interval -= 1000
        if self.timer_interval < 0:
            self.timer_interval = self.set_timer_interval  # Use the stored set timer interval
            self.next_image()

    def load_image_delayed(self):
        # This method will be called after the 500ms delay if no keypress happens
        if self.timer is not None:
            self.root.after_cancel(self.timer)  # Cancel the existing timer
        self.timer = None  # Clear the timer ID
        # Load the current image from history without changing to a new random image
        image_path = os.path.join(self.image_folder, self.history[self.history_index])
        threading.Thread(target=self.load_image, args=(image_path,)).start()
        if not self.is_paused:
            self.timer = self.set_timer_interval
            self.root.after(500, self.start_timer)

    def next_image(self, event=None):
        if self.image_folder != "":
            if len(self.image_files) == len(self.history):
                self.history_index = (self.history_index + 1) % len(self.image_files)
            else:
                self.add_image_to_history()
            self.display_image()
                # Reset the timer interval to the stored set timer interval
        self.timer_interval = self.set_timer_interval
       
        if self.session_active:
            self.session_image_count -= 1
            if self.session_image_count <= 0:
                self.session_index = (self.session_index + 1) % len(self.current_session_list)
                self.session_image_count, interval = self.current_session_list[self.session_index]
                self.timer_interval = interval * 1000  # Setting the new interval from session
            else:
                self.timer_interval = self.set_timer_interval
        else:
            self.timer_interval = self.set_timer_interval
        
    def previous_image(self, event=None):
        if self.image_folder != "" and self.history_index > 0:
            self.history_index -= 1
            self.display_image()

        # When session is active, control the flow based on session settings
        if self.session_active:
            current_count, _ = self.current_session_list[self.session_index]
            # Move to the previous session step, if it's the first image in the current session step
            if self.session_image_count == current_count:
                if self.session_index > 0:
                    self.session_index -= 1
                else:
                    self.session_index = len(self.current_session_list) - 1
                _, interval = self.current_session_list[self.session_index]
                self.session_image_count = self.current_session_list[self.session_index][0]
                self.timer_interval = interval * 1000
            else:
                self.session_image_count += 1
                _, interval = self.current_session_list[self.session_index]
                self.timer_interval = interval * 1000
        else:
            # For regular (non-session) mode, simply reset the timer interval
            self.timer_interval = self.set_timer_interval


when i press next image, sometimes it calls default time (15s)instead. when i set the image timer with the session, it calls sessions correctly but on the second image it suddenly calls for default 15s, then resumes the session. 

i want when i activate session mode and set the timer to use the session, and i open the timer again, i want it to save or acknowledge the previous setting in the session list and session checkbox instead of resetting the timer on opening the timer window.. 

concisely lists me the methods to be changed or added
give me the body methods correction

To correct the `body` method in the `CustomTimeDialog` to prepopulate fields based on the initial session data, you'll need to modify the code to check and apply any passed initial data in the `__init__` method and then reference that data within the `body` method. Here is a suggested approach to make these changes:

First, update the `__init__` method to accept and store `initial_data`:

```python
class CustomTimeDialog(simpledialog.Dialog):
    def __init__(self, parent, initial_data=None):
        self.initial_data = initial_data
        super().__init__(parent)

    # ... other methods ...
```

Then, in the `body` method, you'll need to insert logic to handle the `self.initial_data`:

```python
class CustomTimeDialog(simpledialog.Dialog):
    # ... existing code ...
    
    def body(self, master):
        # ... existing setup code ...

        # Set initial session mode and list if provided
        if self.initial_data:
            initial_active = self.initial_data.get("active", False)
            initial_list = self.initial_data.get("list", [])

            self.togsess_mode.set(initial_active)  # Set the initial state of the session checkbox

            # Update session listbox if there are entries
            if initial_list:
                self.sessionlist = initial_list  # Set the initial session list
                self.sessionString.set(' // '.join(self.sessionlist))  # Update the representation on the Listbox

        # ...remaining setup code...
```

The `self.timebox.bind('<<ListboxSelect>>', self.set_spinboxes_from_selection)` line should be placed after you've finished configuring the `self.timebox` widget, as its purpose is to bind an event handler to the Listbox that will be called when a selection is made.

Here's where you should place the bind statement within your existing `body` method, with markers for clarity:

```python
class CustomTimeDialog(simpledialog.Dialog):
    # ... other methods ...
    
    def body(self, master):
        # ... setup code before this ...

        # [Your initialization code for `self.time_background` and `self.initial_data`]

        # Marker: Line above for initialization code
        # ==========================================

        # Continue with timebox and other widget configurations

        # ==========================================

        # timebox--------------------------------------------------
        ctime_frame = tk.Frame(master, bg=self.time_background)
        ctime_frame.grid(row=1, column=1)
        self.timebox = tk.Listbox(ctime_frame, height=6, width=15, font=self.timebutton_style["font"], bg=self.timebutton_style["bg"], fg="#dddddd", selectbackground="#222222")

        for time_string in ['15s', '30s', '45s', '1m', '5m', '10m']:
            self.timebox.insert(tk.END, time_string)
        self.timebox.grid(row=1, column=1)

        # Bind the event handler here, right after configuring timebox
        self.timebox.bind('<<ListboxSelect>>', self.set_spinboxes_from_selection)

        # ... rest of your code for other widgets ...

        return self.spin_seconds  # initial focus

    # ... other methods ...
```

You should place this bind statement directly after the configuration of `self.timebox`, so any selections made in the Listbox will trigger the `set_spinboxes_from_selection` function.

AttributeError: 'super' object has no attribute 'init'

The error message indicates that there's a typo in the `CustomTimeDialog`'s `__init__` method. You called `super().init(parent)` instead of `super().__init__(parent)`.

In Python, the constructor method is `__init__`, not just `init`, and the missing underscores are causing the error you're seeing. Here's the corrected `__init__` method:

```python
class CustomTimeDialog(simpledialog.Dialog):
    def __init__(self, parent, initial_data=None):
        self.initial_data = initial_data  # Store the initial data
        super().__init__(parent)  # Call the superclass initializer

    # ... the rest of the class ...
```

Remember that when you call `super()`, you're actually referring to the base class of `CustomTimeDialog`, which, according to your code, would likely be `simpledialog.Dialog`. 

`super().__init__(parent)` is the correct way to initialize the parent class. The double underscores before and after `init` are crucial as they denote special methods in Python, often referred to as "dunder" (double-underscore) methods.

The error message indicates that the `CustomTimeDialog` class does not have an attribute `togsess_mode` when the `body` method is called. It appears that in your original code, `self.togsess_mode` is a `tk.BooleanVar()` object that should be initialized before the `body` method is called, probably during the dialog's setup in the `__init__` method.

Here's how you can declare the `self.togsess_mode` before the `body` method:

```python
class CustomTimeDialog(simpledialog.Dialog):
    def __init__(self, parent, initial_data=None):
        self.initial_data = initial_data  # Store the initial data

        # Initialize togsess_mode before calling the superclass's __init__
        self.togsess_mode = tk.BooleanVar(value=False)  # Initialize the BooleanVar here

        super().__init__(parent)  # Call the superclass initializer

    # ... the rest of the class ...

    def body(self, master):
        # ... existing setup code ...

        # Set initial session mode and list if applicable (add it here)
        if self.initial_data:
            initial_active = self.initial_data.get("active", False)
            initial_list = self.initial_data.get("list", [])

            # Now that togsess_mode is initialized, we can safely use it here
            self.togsess_mode.set(initial_active)  # Set the initial state of the session checkbox
            self.toggle_togsess()  # Make sure to apply the toggle state to enable/disable related widgets

            # Update session listbox if there are entries
            if initial_list:
                self.sessionlist = initial_list  # Set the initial session list
                self.sessionString.set(self.sessionlist)  # Update the representation on the Listbox

        # ... the rest of the body method ...

        return self.spin_seconds  # initial focus
```

This will ensure `self.togsess_mode` is available as an attribute of the `CustomTimeDialog` instance when you are trying to access it in the `body` method.

Now, regarding the behavior you described when pressing "Apply", causing another timer window to open: it sounds like the `apply` method might be incorrectly invoking the setting of the timer interval or reopening the dialog. You should ensure that the `apply` method sets the dialog result and does not invoke the timer interval setting directly. Here is what the `apply` method should look like:

```python
def apply(self):
    # Handle applying the dialog state; do not open a new timer window here
    # Set the dialog result to the chosen timer setting or session settings
    # ... your existing logic to handle the result ...
```

Finally, to make sure you are only opening the dialog once, and applying the chosen settings without reopening the dialog, your timer-setting method in the `ImageViewer` class should look something like this:

```python
class ImageViewer:

    def set_timer_interval(self):
        # Pass the current session state to the dialog if there's one active; otherwise pass 'None'
        dialog = CustomTimeDialog(self.root, self.get_current_session_data() if self.session_active else None)
        if dialog.result is not None:
            # ... existing logic to handle the dialog result ...
        self.root.focus_force()  # Refocus on the main ImageViewer window
```

Make sure your code in the `ImageViewer` class properly checks for the dialog result, updates the session settings, and does not automatically initiate another dialog popup. This way, pressing "Apply" should apply the settings and close the dialog without opening a new one.

To solve the `"AttributeError: 'CustomTimeDialog' object has no attribute 'togsess_mode'"` issue, you need to ensure that `self.togsess_mode` is defined in the `CustomTimeDialog` class before you try to access it in the `body` method. To do this, initialize `self.togsess_mode` in the `__init__` method of `CustomTimeDialog`.

Here's how to do it:

```python
class CustomTimeDialog(simpledialog.Dialog):
    def __init__(self, parent, initial_data=None):
        # Initialize toggled session mode variable here
        self.togsess_mode = tk.BooleanVar(value=False)
        # Then store the initial data
        self.initial_data = initial_data
        # Finally, call the constructor of the base (super) class
        super().__init__(parent)

    def body(self, master):
        # ... existing setup code ...
        
        # Since self.togsess_mode is now initialized, you can use it here.
        # Set initial session mode and list if applicable (set here)
        if self.initial_data:
            initial_active = self.initial_data.get("active", False)
            initial_list = self.initial_data.get("list", [])

            self.togsess_mode.set(initial_active)  # Set the initial state of the session checkbox
            
            # The toggle_togsess function should be defined elsewhere in this class.
            # It is responsible for updating the UI state based on the togsess_mode variable.
            self.toggle_togsess()  

            # Update session listbox if there are entries
            if initial_list:
                # Assuming self.sessionlist and self.sessionString are defined in the __init__:
                self.sessionlist = initial_list  # Set the initial session list
                self.sessionString.set(self.sessionlist)  # Update the representation on the Listbox
        
        # ... rest of the body method ...
        
        return self.spin_seconds  # initial focus

    # ... the rest of the CustomTimeDialog class ...
```

In `__init__`, you're creating an instance of `tk.BooleanVar()` that will be used to track the session mode's state, and you're storing it in `self.togsess_mode`. You can now safely call `self.togsess_mode.set(...)` inside the `body` method to set the initial state of the session checkbox.

Please ensure that all the required Tkinter controls, variables, and methods like `self.sessionlist`, `self.sessionString`, and `self.toggle_togsess()` are properly defined and initialized within your `CustomTimeDialog` class according to your overall program structure.
